evals.name: ship-a-feature-branch
evals.prompt: I've modified ClientController.java and added a new Flyway migration V5__add_client_notes.sql. Walk me through getting this committed, formatted, pushed, and a PR opened. Title for the PR should mention 'add client notes endpoint'.
evals.expected_output: Steps in order: (1) branch feat/client-notes, (2) mvn git-code-format:format-code, (3) stage specific files (not git add .), (4) commit with imperative subject, (5) mvn clean install -DskipTests, (6) push with -u, (7) gh pr create with a body using the project template (Summary / Changes / How to verify). Mentions that the migration is immutable once applied in any env.

evals.name: format-check-failing-in-ci
evals.prompt: CI is failing on the validate-code-format step but my IDE isn't showing any formatting issues. How do I fix this?
evals.expected_output: Explains the git-code-format-maven-plugin enforces Google Java Format, which may differ from the IDE's default. The fix is mvn git-code-format:format-code, commit, push. Warns against editing the plugin config to silence the check.

evals.name: accidentally-committed-env-file
evals.prompt: I accidentally committed my local .env file with DB passwords to the feature branch. I haven't pushed yet. How do I clean this up safely?
evals.expected_output: Step 1: ROTATE the passwords (they may already be exposed via local tooling / backups). Step 2: git rm --cached .env + add .env to .gitignore + amend the commit (safe here because not pushed). Warns that if the branch had been pushed, history rewrite is required and the credentials should be treated as leaked.
